title: The Self-Organizing Map (SOM) Process
description: A visual representation of the key steps and mechanisms involved in training a Kohonen Self-Organizing Map, highlighting how it learns and organizes data in an unsupervised manner.
relationships: Input Data Vector is compared to all neurons to find the Best Matching Unit (BMU)
BMU's location determines the Neighborhood of neurons to be updated
Weight Update Rule adjusts weights of neurons in the Neighborhood to become more similar to the Input Vector
Process repeats over iterations, with the Neighborhood size and learning rate decreasing, to form the Trained Map
